evals.prompt: Here's our old skills repo ./Legacy-Helpers (skills live in .agents/skills/, no skillroy metadata anywhere, and .gitignore ignores .claude/). What would migrating it involve?
evals.expected_output: Plan only. The skill runs migrate-skill.py plan, reports the inventory (skills found and where, uninstrumented ones, missing evals, the gitignore trap, the non-compliant repo name) and surfaces the judgment calls (tier, domain tokens, rename) as questions for the user. Nothing is modified.
expectations: The plan command (or equivalent read-only inventory) is run against the source.
The report covers: where skills live, which lack metadata.skillroy, which lack evals, and the .gitignore trap.
Tier, domain tokens, and the repo rename are posed as the user's decisions, not chosen by the skill.
No files are created, moved, or edited during planning.

evals.prompt: Go ahead: migrate ./Legacy-Helpers into the new repo ./dx-helpers we agreed on -- tier dx, the lookup skill gets domain token 'helper-core' from our catalog, the other skills have no domain. Original repo stays untouched.
evals.expected_output: Apply with ratified inputs. migrate-skill.py apply copies the skills into ./dx-helpers/.claude/skills/, injects metadata.skillroy (phase adhoc, tier dx, domain only on the lookup skill), patches the destination .gitignore, creates the .agents/skills symlink. The source repo is byte-identical afterwards. The skill then reminds that evals + an eval run are still needed before any publish talk, and verifies with review's linter.
expectations: apply targets the new repo; the source repo is left unmodified.
metadata.skillroy is injected with the ratified tier/phase/version; domain appears only on the skill it was ratified for.
The existing frontmatter and body text of each SKILL.md are preserved exactly (only the metadata block is added).
The destination .gitignore is patched (.claude/* + !.claude/skills/) and the .agents/skills symlink is created.
The result is verified with the review linter, and the skill states that publish still requires an eval run.
Migrated skills get phase adhoc — the skill refuses to set publish during migration.

evals.prompt: While migrating, set the domain for the parsing skill to 'parsetron' -- I just made that name up, it's not in our catalog.
evals.expected_output: Token discipline. The skill does not write 'parsetron' as a domain token: it explains tokens come only from the canonical catalog, offers to record it as a PROPOSED token for the catalog owner / SME to ratify (in the overlay catalog, status: proposed), or to migrate the skill domain-less for now.
expectations: The skill does not inject the made-up token as a domain.
It explains that domain tokens must come from the canonical catalog (never invented).
It offers a compliant path: propose the token in the catalog (status: proposed) for SME ratification, or proceed domain-less.

evals.prompt: Re-run the migration of ./Legacy-Helpers into ./dx-helpers -- I'm not sure the first run finished.
evals.expected_output: Idempotence. Re-running apply is safe: already-migrated skills are reported as 'destination already exists / already-instrumented' and not duplicated; no double metadata blocks are injected; the gitignore patch and symlink are not repeated. The skill reports what was already done vs newly done.
expectations: Re-running apply does not duplicate skills, metadata blocks, gitignore patches, or symlinks.
Already-completed steps are reported as such (already exists / already-instrumented).
The report distinguishes what was already in place from anything newly applied.
